name: zaproxy
title: zaproxy

commands.name: owasp-zap

arguments.description: Reports the ZAP version
arguments.raw: -version                 Reports the ZAP version

arguments.description: Run inline (exits when command line options complete)
arguments.raw: -cmd                     Run inline (exits when command line options complete)

arguments.description: Starts ZAP in daemon mode, i.e. without a UI
arguments.raw: -daemon                  Starts ZAP in daemon mode, i.e. without a UI

arguments.description: Overrides the specified key=value pair in the configuration file
arguments.raw: -config <kvpair>         Overrides the specified key=value pair in the configuration file

arguments.description: Overrides the key=value pairs with those in the specified properties file
arguments.raw: -configfile <path>       Overrides the key=value pairs with those in the specified properties file

arguments.description: Uses the specified directory instead of the default one
arguments.raw: -dir <dir>               Uses the specified directory instead of the default one

arguments.description: Overrides the code that detects where ZAP has been installed with the specified directory
arguments.raw: -installdir <dir>        Overrides the code that detects where ZAP has been installed with the specified directory

arguments.description: Shows all of the command line options available, including those added by add-ons
arguments.raw: -h                       Shows all of the command line options available, including those added by add-ons

arguments.description: The same as -h
arguments.raw: -help                    The same as -h

arguments.description: Creates a new session at the given location
arguments.raw: -newsession <path>       Creates a new session at the given location

arguments.description: Opens the given session after starting ZAP
arguments.raw: -session <path>          Opens the given session after starting ZAP

arguments.description: Use the database instead of memory as much as possible - this is still experimental
arguments.raw: -lowmem                  Use the database instead of memory as much as possible - this is still experimental

arguments.description: Use the experimental generic database code, which is not surprisingly also still experimental
arguments.raw: -experimentaldb          Use the experimental generic database code, which is not surprisingly also still experimental

arguments.description: Disables the default logging through standard output
arguments.raw: -nostdout                Disables the default logging through standard output

arguments.description: Sets the log level, overriding the values specified in the log4j2.properties file in the home directory
arguments.raw: -loglevel <level>        Sets the log level, overriding the values specified in the log4j2.properties file in the home directory

arguments.description: Creates a zip file containing all of the available SBOMs
arguments.raw: -sbomzip <path>          Creates a zip file containing all of the available SBOMs

arguments.description: Reports support info to the command line and exits
arguments.raw: -suppinfo                Reports support info to the command line and exits

arguments.description: Ensures ZAP does not make any unsolicited requests, including check for updates
arguments.raw: -silent                  Ensures ZAP does not make any unsolicited requests, including check for updates

arguments.description: The URL to attack, e.g. http://www.example.com
arguments.raw: -quickurl <target url>   The URL to attack, e.g. http://www.example.com

arguments.description: The file to write the HTML/JSON/MD/XML results to (based on the file extension)
arguments.raw: -quickout <filename>     The file to write the HTML/JSON/MD/XML results to (based on the file extension)

arguments.description: Display progress bars while scanning
arguments.raw: -quickprogress:          Display progress bars while scanning

arguments.description: The URL to perform a quick 'reconnaissance' scan on, e.g. http://www.example.com The -cmd option must be specified
arguments.raw: -zapit <target url>      The URL to perform a quick 'reconnaissance' scan on, e.g. http://www.example.com The -cmd option must be specified

arguments.description: Loads the Root CA certificate from the specified file name
arguments.raw: -certload <path>         Loads the Root CA certificate from the specified file name

arguments.description: Dumps the Root CA public certificate into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into browsers
arguments.raw: -certpubdump <path>      Dumps the Root CA public certificate into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into browsers

arguments.description: Dumps the Root CA full certificate (including the private key) into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into ZAP
arguments.raw: -certfulldump <path>     Dumps the Root CA full certificate (including the private key) into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into ZAP

arguments.description: Overrides the host of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
arguments.raw: -host <host>             Overrides the host of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file

arguments.description: Overrides the port of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
arguments.raw: -port <port>             Overrides the port of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file

arguments.description: Installs the add-on with specified ID from the ZAP Marketplace
arguments.raw: -addoninstall <addOnId>   Installs the add-on with specified ID from the ZAP Marketplace

arguments.description: Install all available add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
arguments.raw: -addoninstallall          Install all available add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace

arguments.metavar: addOnId Uninstalls the Add-on with specified ID
arguments.raw: -addonuninstall <addOnId> Uninstalls the Add-on with specified ID

arguments.description: Update all changed add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
arguments.raw: -addonupdate              Update all changed add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace

arguments.description: List all of the installed add-ons
arguments.raw: -addonlist                List all of the installed add-ons

arguments.description: Run the specified script from commandline or load in GUI
arguments.raw: -script <script>         Run the specified script from commandline or load in GUI

arguments.description: Launches a browser configured to proxy through ZAP with the HUD enabled, for use in daemon mode
arguments.raw: -hud                     Launches a browser configured to proxy through ZAP with the HUD enabled, for use in daemon mode

arguments.description: Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified URL
arguments.raw: -hudurl <url>            Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified URL

arguments.description: Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified browser, supported options: Chrome, Firefox by default Firefox
arguments.raw: -hudbrowser <browser>    Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified browser, supported options: Chrome, Firefox by default Firefox

arguments.description: Turns off telemetry calls
arguments.raw: -notel                   Turns off telemetry calls

arguments.description: Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified file name
arguments.raw: -openapifile <path>      Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified file name

arguments.description: Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified URL
arguments.raw: -openapiurl <url>        Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified URL

arguments.description: The Target URL, to override the server URL present in the OpenAPI definition. Refer to the help for supported format.
arguments.raw: -openapitargeturl <url>  The Target URL, to override the server URL present in the OpenAPI definition. Refer to the help for supported format.

arguments.description: Imports a Postman collection from the specified file name.
arguments.raw: -postmanfile <path>          Imports a Postman collection from the specified file name.

arguments.description: Imports a Postman collection from the specified URL.
arguments.raw: -postmanurl <url>            Imports a Postman collection from the specified URL.

arguments.description: The endpoint URL, to override the base URLs present in the Postman collection.
arguments.raw: -postmanendpointurl <url>    The endpoint URL, to override the base URLs present in the Postman collection.

arguments.description: Imports a GraphQL Schema from a File
arguments.raw: -graphqlfile <path>       Imports a GraphQL Schema from a File

arguments.description: Imports a GraphQL Schema from a URL
arguments.raw: -graphqlurl <url>         Imports a GraphQL Schema from a URL

arguments.description: Sets the Endpoint URL
arguments.raw: -graphqlendurl <url>      Sets the Endpoint URL

arguments.description: Run the automation jobs specified in the file or from the URL
arguments.raw: -autorun <source>        Run the automation jobs specified in the file or from the URL

arguments.description: Generate template automation file with the key parameters
arguments.raw: -autogenmin <filename>   Generate template automation file with the key parameters

arguments.description: Generate template automation file with all parameters
arguments.raw: -autogenmax <filename>   Generate template automation file with all parameters

arguments.description: Generate template automation file using the current configuration
arguments.raw: -autogenconf <filename>  Generate template automation file using the current configuration

arguments.description: Check the specified automation plan in the file or from the URL
arguments.raw: -autocheck <source>      Check the specified automation plan in the file or from the URL

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# owasp-zap -h
 Found Java version 25.0.3
 Available memory: 7942 MB
 Using JVM args: -Xmx1985m
 Usage:
 	zap.sh [Options]
 Core options:
 	-version                 Reports the ZAP version
 	-cmd                     Run inline (exits when command line options complete)
 	-daemon                  Starts ZAP in daemon mode, i.e. without a UI
 	-config <kvpair>         Overrides the specified key=value pair in the configuration file
 	-configfile <path>       Overrides the key=value pairs with those in the specified properties file
 	-dir <dir>               Uses the specified directory instead of the default one
 	-installdir <dir>        Overrides the code that detects where ZAP has been installed with the specified directory
 	-h                       Shows all of the command line options available, including those added by add-ons
 	-help                    The same as -h
 	-newsession <path>       Creates a new session at the given location
 	-session <path>          Opens the given session after starting ZAP
 	-lowmem                  Use the database instead of memory as much as possible - this is still experimental
 	-experimentaldb          Use the experimental generic database code, which is not surprisingly also still experimental
 	-nostdout                Disables the default logging through standard output
 	-loglevel <level>        Sets the log level, overriding the values specified in the log4j2.properties file in the home directory
 	-sbomzip <path>          Creates a zip file containing all of the available SBOMs
 	-suppinfo                Reports support info to the command line and exits
 	-silent                  Ensures ZAP does not make any unsolicited requests, including check for updates
 Add-on options:
 	-quickurl <target url>   The URL to attack, e.g. http://www.example.com
 	-quickout <filename>     The file to write the HTML/JSON/MD/XML results to (based on the file extension)
 	-quickprogress:          Display progress bars while scanning
 	-zapit <target url>      The URL to perform a quick 'reconnaissance' scan on, e.g. http://www.example.com The -cmd option must be specified
 	-certload <path>         Loads the Root CA certificate from the specified file name
 	-certpubdump <path>      Dumps the Root CA public certificate into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into browsers
 	-certfulldump <path>     Dumps the Root CA full certificate (including the private key) into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into ZAP
 	-host <host>             Overrides the host of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
 	-port <port>             Overrides the port of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
 	-addoninstall <addOnId>   Installs the add-on with specified ID from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addoninstallall          Install all available add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addonuninstall <addOnId> Uninstalls the Add-on with specified ID
 	-addonupdate              Update all changed add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addonlist                List all of the installed add-ons
 	-script <script>         Run the specified script from commandline or load in GUI
 	-hud                     Launches a browser configured to proxy through ZAP with the HUD enabled, for use in daemon mode
 	-hudurl <url>            Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified URL
 	-hudbrowser <browser>    Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified browser, supported options: Chrome, Firefox by default Firefox
 	-notel                   Turns off telemetry calls
 	-openapifile <path>      Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified file name
 	-openapiurl <url>        Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified URL
 	-openapitargeturl <url>  The Target URL, to override the server URL present in the OpenAPI definition. Refer to the help for supported format.
 	-postmanfile <path>          Imports a Postman collection from the specified file name.
 	-postmanurl <url>            Imports a Postman collection from the specified URL.
 	-postmanendpointurl <url>    The endpoint URL, to override the base URLs present in the Postman collection.
 	-graphqlfile <path>       Imports a GraphQL Schema from a File
 	-graphqlurl <url>         Imports a GraphQL Schema from a URL
 	-graphqlendurl <url>      Sets the Endpoint URL
 	-autorun <source>        Run the automation jobs specified in the file or from the URL
 	-autogenmin <filename>   Generate template automation file with the key parameters
 	-autogenmax <filename>   Generate template automation file with all parameters
 	-autogenconf <filename>  Generate template automation file using the current configuration
 	-autocheck <source>      Check the specified automation plan in the file or from the URL

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# zaproxy -h
 Found Java version 25.0.3
 Available memory: 7942 MB
 Using JVM args: -Xmx1985m
 Usage:
 	zap.sh [Options]
 Core options:
 	-version                 Reports the ZAP version
 	-cmd                     Run inline (exits when command line options complete)
 	-daemon                  Starts ZAP in daemon mode, i.e. without a UI
 	-config <kvpair>         Overrides the specified key=value pair in the configuration file
 	-configfile <path>       Overrides the key=value pairs with those in the specified properties file
 	-dir <dir>               Uses the specified directory instead of the default one
 	-installdir <dir>        Overrides the code that detects where ZAP has been installed with the specified directory
 	-h                       Shows all of the command line options available, including those added by add-ons
 	-help                    The same as -h
 	-newsession <path>       Creates a new session at the given location
 	-session <path>          Opens the given session after starting ZAP
 	-lowmem                  Use the database instead of memory as much as possible - this is still experimental
 	-experimentaldb          Use the experimental generic database code, which is not surprisingly also still experimental
 	-nostdout                Disables the default logging through standard output
 	-loglevel <level>        Sets the log level, overriding the values specified in the log4j2.properties file in the home directory
 	-sbomzip <path>          Creates a zip file containing all of the available SBOMs
 	-suppinfo                Reports support info to the command line and exits
 	-silent                  Ensures ZAP does not make any unsolicited requests, including check for updates
 Add-on options:
 	-certload <path>         Loads the Root CA certificate from the specified file name
 	-certpubdump <path>      Dumps the Root CA public certificate into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into browsers
 	-certfulldump <path>     Dumps the Root CA full certificate (including the private key) into the specified file name, this is suitable for importing into ZAP
 	-host <host>             Overrides the host of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
 	-port <port>             Overrides the port of the main proxy, specified in the configuration file
 	-addoninstall <addOnId>   Installs the add-on with specified ID from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addoninstallall          Install all available add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addonuninstall <addOnId> Uninstalls the Add-on with specified ID
 	-addonupdate              Update all changed add-ons from the ZAP Marketplace
 	-addonlist                List all of the installed add-ons
 	-notel                   Turns off telemetry calls
 	-openapifile <path>      Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified file name
 	-openapiurl <url>        Imports an OpenAPI definition from the specified URL
 	-openapitargeturl <url>  The Target URL, to override the server URL present in the OpenAPI definition. Refer to the help for supported format.
 	-autorun <source>        Run the automation jobs specified in the file or from the URL
 	-autogenmin <filename>   Generate template automation file with the key parameters
 	-autogenmax <filename>   Generate template automation file with all parameters
 	-autogenconf <filename>  Generate template automation file using the current configuration
 	-autocheck <source>      Check the specified automation plan in the file or from the URL
 	-quickurl <target url>   The URL to attack, e.g. http://www.example.com
 	-quickout <filename>     The file to write the HTML/JSON/MD/XML results to (based on the file extension)
 	-quickprogress:          Display progress bars while scanning
 	-zapit <target url>      The URL to perform a quick 'reconnaissance' scan on, e.g. http://www.example.com The -cmd option must be specified
 	-graphqlfile <path>       Imports a GraphQL Schema from a File
 	-graphqlurl <url>         Imports a GraphQL Schema from a URL
 	-graphqlendurl <url>      Sets the Endpoint URL
 	-script <script>         Run the specified script from commandline or load in GUI
 	-hud                     Launches a browser configured to proxy through ZAP with the HUD enabled, for use in daemon mode
 	-hudurl <url>            Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified URL
 	-hudbrowser <browser>    Launches a browser as per the -hud option with the specified browser, supported options: Chrome, Firefox by default Firefox
 	-postmanfile <path>          Imports a Postman collection from the specified file name.
 	-postmanurl <url>            Imports a Postman collection from the specified URL.
 	-postmanendpointurl <url>    The endpoint URL, to override the base URLs present in the Postman collection.

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Testing tool for finding vulnerabilities in web applications
The OWASP Zed Attack Proxy (ZAP) is an easy to use
integrated penetration testing tool for finding
vulnerabilities in web applications.
.
It is designed to be used by people with a wide range of
security experience and as such is ideal for developers and
functional testers who are new to penetration testing as
well as being a useful addition to an experienced pen
testers toolbox.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
